figures.caption: Fig. 2. Visualization of a trajectory of our proposed method RYHME-XT versus the baseline DeepONet. The relative ℓ2 errors of the trajectory are 0.0571 for RHYME-XT and 0.4810 for DeepONet, respectively.
Fig. 3. Box plot of the relative ℓ2 error for different time horizons T , with the model being trained on trajectories with T = 50.

Fig. 1. RHYME-XT architecture. The model inputs (u0 and f ) are shown in Purple. Green denotes the basis function generation, parameterized by the FNN hϕ, where its inputs first pass through a random Fourier mapping γ. Red denotes the flow function architecture, taking in the projected inputs to generate the temporal coefficients. Blue denotes the output reconstruction, parameterized by the FNN hu, which combines the basis functions and temporal coefficient to give the approximated solution. Specifically, the inputs u0 and f , are projected using the basis functions to obtain a0 and f(t), respectively. The projected initial condition is mapped to z0 by the encoder henc, after which it is propagated in time through the RNN with input parameters {fk, τk}kt k=0, with kt = 3 in this example. The approximated temporal coefficients â(t) are obtained by passing the output RNN state z through the decoder hdec. These temporal coefficients are combined with the basis functions in the output reconstruction network to obtain the approximated output û(·, t).

Fig. 4. Visualization of a trajectory generated with T = 250 versus model predictions of our proposed method RHYME-XT, trained using trajectories with T = 50. Ground truth: (-), RHYME-XT: (--). The relative ℓ2 error of this trajectory is 0.1173.
